chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 236 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 19.09.2019 Mr. Muhammad Farooq, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Mudassir Qureshi has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable property/asset, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Property attached with the memo of petition, available at page 25, which was left by his deceased father, namely, Zafar Qureshi s/o. Muhammad Fazal Qureshi, who allegedly died on 24.10.2018 at Karachi, leaving behind Mudassir Qureshi (son/petitioner), Mst. Rana Tahir (daughter) and Mubeen Qureshi (son), as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable property are also filed at page 13, 23 and 31 to 41/E. Legal heir No. 2 has filed her affidavit of no objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner. Legal heir No. 3 has filed affidavit of no objection for such purpose and also executed General Power of Attorney in favour of the petitioner, both duly attested by the Deputy Consul General in the Consulate General of Pakistan at Toronto, Canada. Two witnesses, namely, Azra Iqbal wife of Muhammad Iqbal and Khalid Hussain Qureshi s/o. Ishtiaq Hussain Qureshi have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no 2 other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 21.05.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 08.08.2019.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 21.05.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 08.08.2019. Today petitioner and legal heir No. 2 as well as above named witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable property/asset shown in the Schedule of Property, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
